title: Beverages
content:
HOT BEVERAGES

Tea, Coffee, Decaffeinated, Cereal Beverages: Use freshly drawn water; heat rapidly to boiling; boil 1 minute. Add correct proportion of beverage; extract until desired strength. Prolonged steeping brings out bitter flavor and loses aroma.

Chocolate, Cocoa: Must boil 3-5 minutes in water to cook starch thoroughly. Then add to milk heated over boiling water.

Scalded Milk: Heat over hot water until film forms on surface. Stir film into milk when ready to use.

Serving: Serve hot beverages hot or thoroughly chilled. If serving cold, make extra strong and pour over finely crushed ice, or chill diluted beverage. Infusions may be frozen in cubes to chill without further dilution.

Accompaniments:
- Tea: Thinly sliced lemon and orange with whole cloves, candied ginger, mint leaves, rose geranium. Colored sugar cubes. Granulated rainbow sugar in low wide bowl.
- Coffee: Plain cream and sugar; or topped with teaspoon whipped cream. Black coffee preferred by some; few grains of salt added by some.
- Chocolate/Cocoa: Topped with whipped cream or marshmallows.

Demi-Tasse: Strong coffee served in small cups as last course of formal dinner. Served without cream; sugar may be served.

COLD BEVERAGES

Types: Iced coffee, tea, cocoa, chocolate; egg drinks; milk drinks; malted milk; fruit combinations with or without carbonated beverages.

Service: Tall, thin, colorful glasses. Garnish with contrasting color: thin lemon/orange quarters, berries, grapes, mint sprigs, maraschino cherries. Use glass straws with colorful bulbs. Underline glasses with small plates.

Chilling without dilution: Freeze portion of beverage as cubes; use instead of ice. Different color/flavor cubes for visual interest. Freeze fruit (cherries, grapes, citrus slices) in cubes.

Colored Ice: Daintily colored ice cubes lend attractive note to table color scheme.
